problem_70.natural_language: Replacing successive vectors by differences preserves being a basis.
problem_70.lean:
theorem basis_iff_basis_of_diff
    {K V : Type*} [Field K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]
    {n : Nat} (v : Fin n → V) :
    let w : Fin n → V := fun i =>
      if h : (i : ℕ) + 1 < n then
        v i - v ⟨(i : ℕ) + 1, h⟩
      else
        v i
    (LinearIndependent K v ∧ Submodule.span K (Set.range v) = ⊤) ↔
    (LinearIndependent K w ∧ Submodule.span K (Set.range w) = ⊤)
problem_70.natural_language_proof:
Proof: define `w : Fin n → V` by `w i = v i - v (i+1)` when `i+1 < n`, and `w i = v i` for the last
index. The vectors `w` are obtained from `v` by an invertible upper-triangular change of coordinates
with diagonal entries `1`. Therefore the linear spans of `v` and `w` agree, and `w` is linearly
independent iff `v` is. Hence `v` is a basis (linearly independent and spanning) iff `w` is.
